articles.title: Agua, gas o cloacas: más de la mitad de la población carece de al menos uno de estos servicios
articles.content: El 52,9% de la población no accedió al agua corriente, gas de red o cloacas. Otro dato crítico fue la caída de cobertura de obra social, prepaga, mutual o servicio de emergencia al 65,4%, generando una mayor presión sobre el servicio público.

articles.title: Cuenta DNI: cómo ahorrar hasta $24.000 en carnicerías durante abril

articles.title: La economía se hundió 2,6% mensual en febrero, su peor retroceso desde diciembre de 2023
articles.content: La actividad económica se vio afectada por el mal desempeño de sectores clave, como la industria y el comercio, y el fin de la cosecha de trigo. En términos anuales retrocedió 2,1%.

articles.title: El economista que formó a Javier Milei sugirió 3 cambios en el programa económico: "Esto no es sostenible ni durable"
articles.content: Se trata de Roberto Frenkel, referente académico y exprofesor del Presidente. En su análisis, criticó los esquemas basados en endeudamiento externo.

articles.title: Reforma tributaria: Neuquén avisa que puede recibir IVA y bajar la alícuota total
articles.content: El gobernador Rolando Figueroa dijo que los neuquinos están a la altura del desafío. La Casa Rosada tiene que presentar este año un proyecto de ley al Congreso según lo acordado con el FMI.

articles.title: El índice líder mejoró pero aún hay un 85% de chances que en los próximos meses la economía entre en recesión
articles.content: El Índice Líder (IL) crece 0,83% en su versión desestacionalizada durante marzo pero en términos interanuales, el Índice Líder decrece 4,93% con respecto a febrero de 2025.

articles.title: Boom de compras al exterior: importaciones vía courier crecieron 123% en marzo y marcaron récord histórico
articles.content: Los envíos puerta a puerta alcanzaron u$s103 millones y registraron el segundo mayor nivel mensual de la serie. Además del dólar, hubo una mejora relativa del salario medido en moneda dura impulsan el consumo online de productos importados.

articles.title: Mundial 2026: cuánto cuesta reunirse para ver un partido de la Selección argentina
articles.content: Un informe de Worldpanel by Numerator analiza cómo se organizan los argentinos para ver los partidos. Desde dónde se reúnen, hasta qué consumen y qué tan fuerte es el interés por la Selección.

articles.title: Plazo fijo por el piso: así operan los principales bancos hoy, miércoles 22 de abril
articles.content: Rendimientos bajos en pesos obligan a recalcular estrategias mientras los bancos ajustan tasas y los ahorristas buscan no perder frente a la inflación.

articles.title: Tokyo Condo Prices Fall Two Months in Row in Sign Boom May Fade
articles.content: Used condominium prices in central Tokyo fell for second month in March, stoking concerns that Japan’s property boom may be losing momentum.

articles.title: US Stock Futures Decline, Brent Stays Above $100: Markets Wrap
articles.content: US equity-index futures fell in a volatile session, while oil prices traded above $100 a barrel as Middle-East tensions kept markets jittery. Technology stocks gained on robust corporate earnings.

articles.title: Australian Pensions Set to Boost Currency Hedging, Survey Shows
articles.content: The vast majority of Australia’s pension funds intend to shield their portfolios from currency swings amid fears tensions in the Middle East will rapidly escalate, according to a survey by Commonwealth Bank of Australia.

articles.title: Tesla Boosts Spending Plan to $25 Billion in AI, Robotics Push
articles.content: Tesla anticipates billions of dollars in additional spending this year to support Elon Musk’s ambitions to transform the electric-vehicle pioneer into an AI and robotics company. Ed Ludlow reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: NZ Finance Minister Says Recovery Delayed, Not Derailed
articles.content: New Zealand’s nascent economic recovery has been impacted by the war in Iran, but is still expected to get back on track later this year, Finance Minister Nicola Willis said.

articles.title: Indonesia Sells ¥172.1 Billion Samurai Bond on Strong Demand
articles.content: Indonesia raised ¥172.1 billion ($1.1 billion) in its biggest Samurai bond sale in two years as strong investor demand for its credit outweighed concerns about budgetary pressures due to the Middle East war.

articles.title: Counterpoint: SK Hynix is Riding the Supercycle
articles.content: Tom Kang, Research Director at Counterpoint, discusses the outlook for SK Hynix as the company reported a five-fold jump in quarterly profit and reiterated plans to ratchet up its capex this year. He speaks with Haidi Stroud-Watts and Paul Allen on Bloomberg: The Asia Trade. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: GSAM's Calnon on Markets, Investment Strategy
articles.content: Greg Calnon, Global Head of Public Investing at Goldman Sachs Asset Management, discusses his outlook for markets and investment strategy. He speaks with Haidi Stroud-Watts and Paul Allen on Bloomberg: The Asia Trade.  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Alibaba-Backed Zelos Is Said to Plan Hong Kong IPO to Raise About $600 Million
articles.content: Chinese robovan operator Zelos Technology is planning an initial public offering in Hong Kong that could raise about $600 million, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.content: Gotion High-tech Founder Li Zhen tells Bloomberg about the battery maker's growth outlook. He also discusses US expansion plans, the Iran war's impact on supply chains, and fierce industry "involution." He spoke exclusively with Bloomberg's Minmin Low. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Indonesia Rupiah Falls Most in Seven Months on Rising Oil Prices
articles.content: The Indonesian rupiah declined by the most in seven months as rising oil prices hurt sentiment for the nation’s assets.

articles.title: Copper Falls From Highest Since February With Iran War in Limbo
articles.content: Copper fell with other metals as investors watched for the next major developments from the Middle East after peace talks stalled, with the US and Iran both vying for control of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: No Deadline for Iran Proposal Amid Hormuz Standoff: US
articles.content: The US and Iran are locked in a battle for control of the Strait of Hormuz after failing to meet for a fresh round of peace talks, with both sides blocking the waterway to gain leverage during an extended ceasefire. Bloomberg's Joumanna Bercetche reports from Dubai. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: AI Boom Sparks Rush Into Chinese Optical Stocks as Top Trade
articles.content: Chinese optical stocks are attracting growing investor interest on expectations that rising demand for optical components critical to artificial intelligence will drive the next leg of sector outperformance.

articles.title: Oil Extends Gain as Peace Negotiations Between US and Iran Stall
articles.content: Oil rose for a fourth day as the US and Iran remained locked in a battle for control of the Strait of Hormuz after failing to meet for a fresh round of peace talks.

articles.title: SoftBank Seeks $10 Billion Loan Backed by OpenAI Shares
articles.content: SoftBank Group Corp. is seeking a $10 billion loan secured by its shares in US artificial intelligence giant OpenAI, people familiar with the matter said, as it takes on more debt for its push into AI. Bloomberg's Trista Xinyi Luo reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Gold Falls as US-Iran Maritime Standoff Heightens Inflation Risk
articles.content: Gold declined as the US extension of a ceasefire with Iran left global markets grappling with a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, impacting energy security and keeping inflation risks high.

articles.title: Stocks Fall With Bonds as Iran Concerns Boost Oil: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Stocks and bonds fell as stalled US-Iran talks and the continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz drove oil prices higher, souring risk sentiment after a record rally on Wall Street.

articles.title: JPMorgan to Add Philippine Bonds to Key EM Index in 2027
articles.content: JPMorgan Chase & Co. said it will add local-currency bonds from the Philippines into its widely followed emerging-market index early next year, a move that may draw foreign investment and help spur the nation’s economy.

articles.title: SET President: IPO Pipeline Faces a Difficult Situation

articles.title: France Probes Weather Data Glitch After Surge in Polymarket Bets
articles.content: France’s forecasting office flagged suspected tampering with weather sensors at the country’s largest airport and referred the case to police, after detecting unusual readings alongside heavy betting on a popular prediction market.

articles.title: Roknifard: US Blockade Won't Yield Significant Results
articles.content: The US and Iran remain locked in a standoff over the Strait of Hormuz, with both sides effectively choking off traffic during an extended ceasefire. President Donald Trump said the truce agreed to on April 7 would stay in place indefinitely while Tehran says it has no plans to take part in negotiations imminently. Julia Roknifard, Senior Lecturer at the School of Law & Governance at Taylor's University in Malaysia spoke to Bloomerg’s Horizons Middle East and Africa anchor Joumanna Bercetche on the likely scenario. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Sainsbury’s Says Middle East Conflict Risks Hitting Profit
articles.content: J Sainsbury Plc said profit could slip this year as Britain’s second-largest grocer faces higher costs and consumer uncertainty triggered by the conflict in the Middle East.

articles.title: Apollo Nears €1.4 Billion Deal for Forvia’s Interior Unit
articles.content: Apollo Global Management Inc. is nearing a deal to acquire the auto interiors business of Forvia SE, people familiar with the matter said.

articles.title: Roche Sales Decline on Swiss Franc’s Strength, Ocrevus Miss
articles.content: Roche Holding AG’s sales fell in the first quarter, hurt by the Swiss franc’s appreciation against the dollar and lower-than-expected demand for a key multiple sclerosis medicine.

articles.title: The China Show 4/23/2026 (Video)
Azour: Prolonged War Impact Deeper Than Oil and Gas
articles.content: The IMF's Middle East and Central Asia Director says a prolonged war and continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz could deepen economic disruptions well beyond oil and gas.  Jihad Azour spoke to Joumanna Bercetche about how the shock is reverberating across the Gulf and broader global trade. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: India Contracts Fertilizer at Nearly Double Pre-War Price
articles.content: India, the world’s top importer of urea, has booked the nitrogen-based fertilizer at sharply higher prices than in a previous tender as the Middle East conflict disrupts supplies and pushes global benchmarks higher.

articles.title: Yuan to Surpass Yen in Currency Options Trading, LCH Says
articles.content: The yuan is on track to overtake the yen as the second-most traded currency against the dollar in the foreign-exchange options market, according to London-based clearing house LCH.

articles.title: German Business Activity Unexpectedly Shrinks as Services Plunge
articles.content: German private-sector activity unexpectedly contracted as the Iran war triggered the biggest plunge in the services sector in more than three years.

articles.content: The reflex to buy stocks is kicking back into gear.

articles.title: Taiwan to Ease Limits on Active Funds’ Investments in TSMC
articles.content: Taiwan’s financial regulator plans to ease the limit on how much funds can invest in a single stock, lifting an impediment that has prevented local money managers from taking full advantage of the surge in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.’s shares in recent years.

articles.title: Honeywell Is Said to Near Sale of Warehouse Services Unit to AIP

articles.content: Blackstone Inc. reported a larger-than-expected jump in distributable earnings, boosted by a robust start to dealmaking before the war in Iran rattled investors. With US markets rising and AI ventures preparing to go public, President Jon Gray predicted the firm’s “best year ever” for stock listings.

articles.title: Anglo Advances Coal Business Sale After Setback With Peabody
articles.content: Anglo American Plc has at least three potential buyers for its Australian steelmaking coal business after a sale to Peabody Energy Corp. was derailed last year following a fire at one of the mines.

articles.title: US Navy Secretary Fired After Infighting at Pentagon: Sources | The Pulse 4/23
articles.content:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests:
Hugh Gimber, JPMorgan Asset Management, Global Market Strategist; Fawaz Gerges, London School of Economics, International Relations Professor; Stirling Larkin, Foxtail Pine, CEO. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.content: Aliko Dangote, Africa’s richest man, pledged to help East African nations build a new oil refinery in Tanzania, as the war in Iran exposes the continent’s over-reliance on fuel imports from the Middle East.

articles.title: Keurig Dr Pepper Rises as Cold Drinks Drives Earnings Beat
articles.content: Keurig Dr Pepper Inc. shares rose after revenue and earnings beat expectations as strong sales of cold beverages and in international markets offset a decline in coffee.

articles.title: Air France-KLM, Lufthansa Bids Advance in Race for TAP Stake
articles.content: Portugal has invited Air France-KLM and Deutsche Lufthansa AG to submit binding bids for a minority stake in TAP SA, setting up a contest for one of Europe’s last remaining state-owned airlines.

articles.title: Coal’s Crown Is Slowly Slipping Despite US Support
articles.content: Global coal-power generation edged lower last year, while electricity from renewables jumped.

articles.title: Oil to Be ‘Ongoing, Absolute Disaster’ Over Next Two Months, Analyst Sankey Says
articles.content: “The situation here gets worse, simply because it’s guaranteed,” says Paul Sankey, president at Sankey Research. He explains the tough times ahead for the global oil market over the next two to three months, even if the Strait of Hormuz were to reopen in the next few days. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Big Bet on Orban’s Exit Came From Center of His Family’s Empire
articles.content: An asset-management fund in the Equilor group of companies part-owned by Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s son-in-law profited from financial-market bets on the strongman losing power.

articles.title: BlackRock’s Shigekawa Sees Risk to Yen on BOJ Miscommunication
articles.content: BlackRock Inc. portfolio manager Rie Shigekawa highlighted risks to the yen if the Bank of Japan fails to prepare the market for a June interest-rate hike at the next policy meeting.

articles.title: Japan’s Katayama Reiterates Constant Close Contact With US on FX
articles.content: Japan’s Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said officials are in close contact around the clock with their US counterparts on the foreign exchange market, as Tokyo remains on alert over speculative moves that are keeping the yen weak.

articles.title: Iran War: Stocks Fall as Oil Climbs With Talks in Limbo | The Opening Trade 4/23/2026
articles.content:
A record-breaking rally in US stocks came to a halt as peace talks in the Middle East remained in limbo, sending Brent crude further above $100 a barrel.

Futures for the S&P 500 fell 0.4%. The US benchmark hit an all-time high in the previous session and extended gains for April to more than 9% following a slew of strong corporate results and the longest-ever advance in chipmakers. Brent rose 0.7% to around $102.60 a barrel as the US and Iran kept blocking the Strait of Hormuz after failing to meet for fresh talks.

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson and Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: AllianceBernstein Shuts Hedge Fund Arya on Lack of Scale
articles.content: AllianceBernstein Holding LP has shut its hedge fund AB Arya, the latest sign of how a lack of scale is hindering challengers to the larger multi-strat firms.

articles.title: Japan's Katayama on Constant Close Contact With US on FX
articles.content: Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama says that she is in close communication with the US on FX. Katayama spoke exclusively with Shery Ahn at the Bloomberg New Voices 2026 event in Tokyo. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: US Stock Rally Stalls With Iran Talks in Limbo: Markets Wrap
articles.content: A record-breaking rally in US stocks came to a halt as peace talks in the Middle East remained in limbo, keeping Brent crude above $100 a barrel.

articles.title: Japan's Katayama on Iran Response
articles.content: Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama says she is monitoring closely for developments on Iran. Katayama spoke exclusively with Shery Ahn at the Bloomberg New Voices 2026 event in Tokyo. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: BlackRock’s Shigekawa Sees Risk to Yen on BOJ Miscommunication
articles.content: There could be risks to the yen if the Bank of Japan fails to prepare the market for a June interest-rate hike at the next policy meeting, according to BlackRock's Japan Managing Director and Portfolio Manager. Speaking at a panel moderated by Lisa Du at the 2026 Bloomberg New Voices Tokyo event, Rie Shigekawa told Lisa Du that market expectations for the central bank to raise rates next week have diminished since the Middle East conflict broke out. Former BOJ board member and Keio University Economics Professor Sayuri Shirai also said that if the market concludes that the BOJ is falling behind the curve and unwilling to raise rates, that would be a trigger for a cheaper yen.  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: JPMorgan Readies Fresh Private Credit Push After Needling Market
articles.content: After years weighing how to dive deeper into private credit, JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s $4.3 trillion asset manager is committing to a strategy that will plow tens of billions of dollars into loans sourced by the firm’s commercial bankers.

articles.title: Hormuz Tensions Rise as US, Iran Seek Leverage
articles.content: Iranian state TV aired what it says is a video of Iran's navy seizing vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, while the US said it intercepted two Iranian supertankers trying to evade its blockade, as talks to end the war remained in limbo. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Business Costs Surging Amid Iran War, Says JPMorgan Strategist
articles.content: Business activity in the euro area unexpectedly shrank for the first time since late 2024 due to a steep drop in the services sector as the Iran war weighs on consumers. The Composite PMI compiled by S&P Global declined to 48.6 in April from 50.7 the previous month, dropping below the 50 threshold separating growth from contraction. In the UK PMI data showed that the private sector quickly bounced back from a slowdown caused by the Iran war as firms rushing to secure supplies and price pressures mounting. Hugh Gimber, Global Market Strategist, JP Morgan joined Stephen Carroll on Bloomberg Radio to discuss. he says business costs are 'rising sharply.' (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: PE firm L Catterton launches fund with top athletes
articles.content: LVMH-backed group will seek to harness influence of sports personalities to enhance portfolio companies

articles.title: Plazo fijo siguen sin subir: así operan los principales bancos hoy, jueves 23 de abril
articles.content: Las entidades mantienen rendimientos estables pese al contexto inflacionario y la política monetaria actual, con diferencias leves entre ofertas.

articles.title: La confianza del consumidor se desplomó casi 6% en marzo
articles.content: El Índice de Confianza del Consumidor cayó 5,7% frente a marzo y retrocedió más de 10% interanual, según la Universidad Di Tella. El deterioro fue más fuerte en hogares de menores ingresos y en la predisposición para comprar bienes durables e inmuebles.

articles.title: Dólar e inflación: qué esperan los gurúes financieros para lo que queda del 2026
articles.content: Más de 40 economistas relevados por FocusEconomics prevén un tipo de cambio de $1.699 para diciembre, por debajo de estimaciones previas. Al mismo tiempo, elevaron la proyección de inflación anual al 29%. Algunos bancos incluso ven al dólar cerrando por debajo del valor actual.

articles.title: US Stocks Decline as US-Iran Impasse Continues, Oil Price Jumps
articles.content: US stocks stepped back from record highs on Thursday as the stalemate between Washington and Tehran continued while investors pored over corporate earnings.

articles.title: American Airlines Lowers Guidance Due to Rising Fuel Prices
articles.content: Higher fuel prices due to the war in Iran are weighing on American Airlines. The carrier lowered its full-year earnings target even after beating on earnings for the quarter. Bloomberg's Siddharth Phillip reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Apollo’s Ingenico Starts Talks With Lenders on ‘Untenable’ Debt
articles.content: French payments firm Ingenico, backed by Apollo Global Management, has started debt talks with creditors as it struggles to keep up with its interest bill.

articles.title: Banks Kick Off Talks on Lone Star’s €1.5 Billion Lonza Unit Debt
articles.content: Banks are preparing to sell €1.5 billion ($1.75 billion) of debt financing backing Lone Star Funds’ acquisition of Lonza Group AG’s capsules and health ingredients business.

articles.title: Jay Leno Is Unusual Guest in Muni Roadshow for Airport Deal
articles.content: Jay Leno’s latest hosting gig involves a classic car, an airport construction site and municipal bonds.

articles.title: Andurand Lost 52% in April First Half as Ceasefire Hurt Oil Bets
articles.content: Energy trader Pierre Andurand’s largest hedge fund plunged about 52% in the first half of April, wiping out first quarter gains made on bullish oil bets at the start of the Iran war.

articles.title: Vail Resorts Sees Earnings at Low End of View on Dearth of Snow
articles.content: Vail Resorts, Inc. said its fiscal-year earnings will be at the low end of guidance after poor snowfall led to a slump in total skier visits to its North American resorts this season.

articles.title: US Jobless Claims Rise to 214,000 With Layoffs Limited
articles.content: Applications for US unemployment benefits increased by 6,000 to 214,000 last week but remain at a level consistent with low layoffs. Michael McKee reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Stocks Fall as Hormuz Standoff Drives Oil Higher: Markets Wrap
articles.content: A flare-up in tensions over the Strait of Hormuz sent stocks lower as oil rose on concern that a prolonged closure of the waterway could worsen energy disruptions, boosting inflation and slowing economic growth.

articles.title: New Mountain Raises $2.4 Billion to Hold Azuria Water for Longer
articles.content: New Mountain Capital collected $2.4 billion to extend its hold on infrastructure firm Azuria Water Solutions, underscoring the booming market for secondhand private equity stakes.

articles.title: La industria autopartista arrancó 2026 en retroceso, pero muestra señales de rebote mensual
articles.content: El sector acumula una fuerte caída interanual en el primer bimestre, aunque febrero exhibe una recuperación significativa frente a enero. Las cifras reflejan el impacto de la menor producción automotriz, el retroceso en exportaciones y un mercado interno debilitado.

articles.title: Exportaciones: Buenos Aires lideró en el primer trimestre y Neuquén ganó peso por el boom energético
articles.content: Las ventas externas sumaron US$21.853 millones entre enero y marzo. Buenos Aires, Santa Fe y Córdoba concentraron los mayores montos exportados, mientras Neuquén se destacó por el fuerte avance del sector energético.

articles.title: El mayor fondo soberano del mundo perdió u$s135.000 millones en el primer trimestre
articles.content: La caída de las acciones tecnológicas en Wall Street y la apreciación de la corona noruega golpearon el portafolio del fondo petrolero de Noruega, que acumuló pérdidas por 1,27 billones de coronas entre enero y marzo.

articles.title: El dólar oficial sube por segunda jornada consecutiva y el minorista ya supera los $1.400
articles.content: En el segmento mayorista, el tipo de cambio se sostiene por debajo de los $1.390.

articles.title: El espejismo contable: la trampa de la deuda flotante detrás del ancla fiscal
articles.content: El Gobierno exhibe superávit, pero los datos de la Oficina de Presupuesto del Congreso revelan una acumulación insostenible de pagos atrasados. Un equilibrio macroeconómico temporal con fecha de caducidad.

articles.title: El Gobierno pisó pagos por $2 billones en marzo para cerrar la revisión del FMI
articles.content: Así pudo cerrar las cuentas ante el FMI. Además de ello este año está contabilizando ingresos de privatizaciones. La recaudación fiscal cayó 8% en el primer trimestre.

articles.title: Nueva señal sobre el deterioro económico: la confianza del consumidor se desplomó casi 6% en marzo
articles.content: El Índice de Confianza del Consumidor retrocedió más de 10% interanual, según la Universidad Di Tella y sumó las primeras tres bajas consecutivas desde abril de 2022. El deterioro fue más fuerte en hogares de menores ingresos y en la predisposición para comprar bienes durables e inmuebles.

articles.title: Warner Bros. Shareholders Approve $110 Billion Paramount Deal

articles.title: Blackstone's Jon Gray says AI is Single Biggest Driver | Open Interest 4/23/2026
articles.content: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." Crude surges as tensions escalate in the Strait of Hormuz, rattling global markets. Blackstone’s Jon Gray doubles down, calling for the firm’s “best year ever” as IPO momentum builds. Plus, a wave of earnings—Tesla ramps AI spending to $25 billion, American Airlines braces for soaring fuel costs, and American Express sees travel demand cool. ServiceNow’s Bill McDermott says growth is still the story. (Source: Bloomberg)
